title: Paspalum ostenii

It belongs to the genus Paspalum, which consists of various species of grasses found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the genus Paspalum, Paspalum ostenii is further categorized under the tribe Paniceae. intro: Paspalum ostenii, also known as P. ostenii, is a perennial grass native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of P. ostenii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with P. ostenii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to relieve respiratory congestion and coughs.
